fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Alabama Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Alabama - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity alabama, how to transfer occupational therapist license to alabama, occupational therapist compact alabama, alabama occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: Alabama State Board of Occupational Therapy
board.mailing_address:
Alabama State Board of Occupational Therapy
RSA Union Building
100 North Union Street, Suite 570
Montgomery, AL 36104-3701

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT)

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in Alabama.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Register for an Account and Submit Online Application
steps.description: Navigate to the Alabama State Board of Occupational Therapy's Online Services Portal, register for a new account, then log in to complete and submit the initial application for Occupational Therapist licensure.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee of $100 as part of the online application process.

steps.title: Request Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for your official academic transcripts, verifying graduation from an ACOTE-accredited occupational therapy program, to be sent directly from your educational institution to the Alabama State Board of Occupational Therapy.

steps.title: Submit Fieldwork Verification
steps.description: Ensure your fieldwork supervisor directly submits verification of your supervised fieldwork experience to the Board, confirming completion of all required hours.

steps.title: Pass NBCOT Examination and Send Scores
steps.description: Successfully pass the National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) examination and request your official scores to be sent directly from NBCOT to the Alabama State Board of Occupational Therapy.

steps.title: Pay Initial Licensure Fee and Await Issuance
steps.description: Once your application and all supporting documents are approved by the Board, pay the initial licensure fee of $100. Your license will then be issued.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBCOT exam. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Alabama OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around  to . Check the specific fee breakdown.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does Alabama have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: Alabama has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, occupational therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to Alabama?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Alabama while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

hero.title: Become a Occupational Therapist in Alabama
hero.dek: To apply for an Occupational Therapist license in Alabama, applicants must submit an electronic application through the Alabama State Board of Occupational Therapy's Online Services Portal.

official_links.registration_steps: Go to the Alabama State Board of Occupational Therapy's Online Services Portal and select 'Register for an Account' to create your user profile, then log in to begin your application.
